author: lOrm
content:
VERIFIED FIXED — existing dispatch_subagent_sync (src/subagent.rs:1439-1465) fully addresses all three #425 concerns.

Forensic evidence (src/subagent.rs @ HEAD d151118a):

1. block_in_place panic on current_thread → ERRORS CLEANLY, no panic.
   Line 1444-1457: matches Handle::try_current().runtime_flavor():
     - RuntimeFlavor::MultiThread (line 1446) → block_in_place + block_on (safe).
     - _ (CurrentThread + future variants) (line 1449) → returns typed error message "Task tool dispatched from a current_thread tokio runtime: cannot block_on without deadlock..." with is_err=true. NO panic, NO deadlock.

2. New Runtime inside existing runtime → AVOIDED.
   Line 1459: Runtime::new() only runs in the Err(_) arm of Handle::try_current() — i.e. only when there is provably no runtime in scope (CLI/test boundary). Anti-pattern of nested runtimes is impossible.

3. Shared globals BACKGROUND_AGENTS + TRANSCRIPT_STORE remain safe.
   - BACKGROUND_AGENTS (src/subagent.rs:443): LazyLock<BackgroundAgentManager> with internal sync primitives.
   - TRANSCRIPT_STORE (src/subagent.rs:459): LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<...>>>.
   Both use cross-thread-safe primitives, not runtime-bound state, so concurrent access from CLI runtime vs. background tokio runtime is sound.

Test coverage pinning all three dispatch branches (src/subagent.rs):
  - issue719_current_thread_runtime_returns_error_without_panicking (line 2103) — asserts typed error, no panic.
  - issue719_multi_thread_runtime_dispatches_to_run_subagent (line 2137) — asserts reaches run_subagent via resume fast-fail.
  - issue719_no_runtime_creates_runtime_and_dispatches (line 2168) — asserts Runtime::new() fallback succeeds.

Verification: cargo test --lib subagent::tests::issue719 → 3 passed, 0 failed.
Baseline preserved: cargo test --lib → 1573 passed, 0 failed.
Clippy clean: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features → exit 0.

Closing as verified-fixed; no additional commit required.
